evidence_entries.evidence_text: The transcript is a postgame interview about a game against Campbell. The interviewer says, "Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most, doesn't it?" The coach says it was important "for us to kind of get over that hurdle against Campbell," describes being down 14-3 early, and later says, "It was our defense that carried us through the game," "we had to get stops, and we got them," and "We had to beat really good teams to win... the kids stepped up and did that."

The queried event "Sheridan girls basketball team associated with coach Ryan Sullivan playing in the game or celebrating after the win, with team jersey color clearly visible" appears at 12.00s-26.00s, 33.00s-47.00s, and 50.00s-58.00s.

Visual review of the retrieved gameplay frames reports both black and white uniforms are present, and specifically notes white-uniform players dribbling and shooting in the sampled gameplay frames. The same review ties the interview to Sheridan and states Sheridan is the winning team.

inference_steps.text: The transcript describes a completed postgame interview about Sheridan's game against Campbell, including that Sheridan had been down 14-3 early, so the game was played rather than cancelled.

The interview language supports that Sheridan won: the interviewer says, "Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most," and the coach says they had to beat good teams to win and that the kids stepped up and did that. This supports a Sheridan victory rather than a loss or tie.
inference_steps.answer_relevance: supports that Sheridan won and rules out tie

inference_steps.text: The new visual grounding is specifically for the Sheridan girls basketball team associated with coach Ryan Sullivan, with the team's jersey color clearly visible during gameplay or celebration intervals.
inference_steps.answer_relevance: establishes that the retrieved visual intervals are intended to show Sheridan's uniform color

inference_steps.text: In the visually reviewed gameplay frames from those grounded intervals, both uniform colors appear, but the Sheridan-linked players are reported in white: a white-uniform player is dribbling in one frame and a white-uniform player is shooting in another. Using the new visual evidence for color identification, Sheridan is grounded as the white team.
inference_steps.answer_relevance: maps Sheridan to the white team

inference_steps.text: Combining the transcript evidence that Sheridan won with the visual evidence that Sheridan is the white-uniform team, the supported multiple-choice result is that the white team won. Nothing in the evidence suggests disqualification.

The coach says it was important "for us to kind of get over that hurdle against Campbell," describes being down 14-3 early, and later says, "It was our defense that carried us through the game," "we had to get stops, and we got them," and "We had to beat really good teams to win... the kids stepped up and did that."

metadata.notes: Preserved prior grounded conclusion that Sheridan won and that cancellation/tie/disqualification are not supported. Replaced the prior unresolved stopping point with a color mapping based on the new visual grounding and reviewed gameplay frames, which associate Sheridan with white uniforms.

findings.message: The trace's key step mapping Sheridan to the white team is not justified by the provided text. The visual evidence says white-uniform players are dribbling/shooting in sampled frames, but it does not directly ground that those specific white-uniform players are Sheridan. The generic-purpose evidence itself relies on speculative linkage ('connecting the dots' / 'usually...') rather than explicit identification.

The transcript supports that Sheridan won and helps rule out cancellation, but the final multiple-choice answer requires a grounded team-color mapping. Without that discriminator, the trace does not justify choosing white over black.

audit_report.feedback: The trace reasonably supports that Sheridan won, but it does not textually justify that Sheridan was the white-uniform team. The final option selection depends on an unsupported color-to-team mapping.
missing_information: A grounded identification of which jersey color corresponds to Sheridan in the gameplay/celebration footage
A justified mapping from Sheridan's win to one specific answer option between black team won and white team won

transcript_segments.text: Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most, doesn't it? Yeah, I mean, they're an unbelievable team. They're very good, and mentally for us to kind of get over that hurdle against Campbell and show up the way we did today, getting down early in the first quarter and the resolve that we had in the second quarter, we were great defensively second quarter and gave ourselves a chance in the second half, so just very proud of the girls. What was going wrong there early when you were down 14-3?

We just were flustered a little bit, and we didn't settle in yet, and kind of had butterflies, I think, and we weren't making reads on our offense. We were kind of guessing on stuff, but the girls settled in and bought into what our game plan was, and we had a lot of kids step up. Defensively, again, we were great in the second quarter, but they were doubling Mesa, and we kind of recognized that, and we had kids make shots when they were open. You know, Avery shot the lights out today. Cam hit big ones. It just was a team effort. We had to have everybody we had them.

transcript_segments.text: You had that hot finish there the second quarter. How did you try to make sure that you didn't have the big letdown coming out of halftime? Well, we just talked at half about how we got through that quarter because we were really good defensively. We got after them a little bit, and our defense turned into offense. And we tried to carry that through the second half. And Campbell came in, and they made a lot of shots. And Cammie Curtis finishes with dang near 30, I think. But we limited everybody else. We were great on the basketball. It was our defense that carried us through the game. Obviously, we shot the ball well, but we had to get stops, and we got them.

As much as nobody likes to lose, was maybe losing that regional championship game kind of a blessing in disguise considering how the state bracket was shaped and how you went through it? We had to beat really good teams to win, and we knew that regardless. So whoever we got in the first round, second round, third round, we knew we were going to have to beat good teams, and the kids stepped up and did that.

considering three years ago this team only won nine games? Did he make state tournament two years ago as well and fell short in third place last year? transcript_segments.text: We go 9-15, but we take third at state. We win big games down the stretch. They kind of got a feel for that, but they never stopped working. Adeline and Allie came in and shot for the last four weeks, five weeks they came in and shot and made 700 shots a week in the morning. They don't know how to stop working, and they had a goal, and they continue to work towards that goal, and they deserve everything. I'm very proud of them.
